title: Hymenidium amabile
taxonomy: Hymenidium amabile is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Hymenidium and is a member of the order Apiales. The plant is native to the Himalayan region and is also found in other parts of Asia. Its classification is further supported by its botanical characteristics, including its leaf structure and flower morphology. As a member of the Apiaceae family, it is closely related to other plants in this family, such as carrots and parsley.
